name: Infrastructure as Code ‚Äî Ansible
description: Ansible playbook conventions, idempotency rules, inventory management, and secrets handling.

sections.title: Ansible Playbook Conventions
sections.content:
## Ansible Playbook Conventions

### Directory Structure

Follow the official Ansible best-practice layout:

```
ansible/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ inventories/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ production/
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ hosts.yml
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ group_vars/
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ staging/
‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ hosts.yml
‚îÇ       ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ group_vars/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ roles/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ common/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ webserver/
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ database/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ playbooks/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ site.yml
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ webservers.yml
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ databases.yml
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ansible.cfg
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ requirements.yml
```

### Playbook Rules

1. **Idempotency is mandatory.** Every task must be safe to run multiple times. Use `state:` parameters, `creates:`, or `when:` guards to ensure this.
2. One playbook, one concern. Do not write a single playbook that configures ten different services.
3. Use **roles** for any logic that spans more than one playbook or host group.
4. Always set `become: false` at the play level and use `become: true` only at the task level where root is actually required.
5. Pin Ansible collection versions in `requirements.yml`. Run `ansible-galaxy install -r requirements.yml` in CI.
6. Use `tags:` on tasks and plays so that subsets can be executed in isolation.

### Task Writing

- Prefer Ansible built-in modules over `shell:` or `command:`. Built-in modules are idempotent; shell commands usually are not.
- When `shell:` or `command:` is unavoidable, add `changed_when: false` (if it never changes state) or a meaningful `changed_when` expression.
- Always include a `name:` for every task ‚Äî use imperative sentences: "Ensure nginx is installed".
- Use `block:` / `rescue:` / `always:` for error handling in complex task sequences.

sections.title: Ansible Variable Management
sections.content:
## Ansible Variable Management

### Variable Precedence & Organisation

Use Ansible's variable precedence intentionally:

| Source | Precedence | Use For |
|--------|-----------|--------|
| `defaults/main.yml` in roles | Lowest | Safe defaults anyone can override |
| `group_vars/all/` | Low | Org-wide defaults |
| `group_vars/{group}/` | Medium | Environment/group-specific values |
| `host_vars/{host}/` | High | Host-specific overrides |
| Extra vars (`-e`) | Highest | CI/CD pipeline injections only |

### Naming Conventions

- Role variables: `rolename_variable_name` (prefix with role name to avoid collisions)
- Boolean variables: use `true`/`false` (not `yes`/`no` or `1`/`0`)
- All variable names: `snake_case`
- Do not reuse variable names across roles without the role prefix

### Secrets Handling

- **Never** store plaintext secrets in inventory files or group_vars committed to source control.
- Use **Ansible Vault** for sensitive variables. Vault-encrypted files use the `.vault.yml` suffix.
- Vault passwords are injected at runtime via `--vault-password-file` pointing to a file sourced from a secrets manager, not hardcoded.
- Rotate vault passwords as part of your key rotation policy (at minimum annually).
- Alternatively, integrate with HashiCorp Vault using the `community.hashi_vault` collection for dynamic secret injection.

sections.title: Ansible Role Testing
sections.content:
## Ansible Role Testing

### Molecule

Every role must have a Molecule test scenario:

```
roles/webserver/
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ molecule/
    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ default/
        ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ molecule.yml
        ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ converge.yml
        ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ verify.yml
```

- `converge.yml` ‚Äî applies the role
- `verify.yml` ‚Äî uses `ansible.builtin.assert` or Testinfra to validate the expected state
- Run `molecule test` in CI for every pull request that modifies a role

### Lint

- Run `ansible-lint` on all playbooks and roles in CI. Treat warnings as errors on `main`.
- Use `yamllint` to enforce YAML formatting (2-space indent, max line length 160).
- Validate syntax with `ansible-playbook --syntax-check` for all playbooks.

### CI Pipeline Steps

```
1. ansible-lint
2. yamllint
3. ansible-playbook --syntax-check
4. molecule test (per role)
5. Integration test in staging environment
```
